data.content:
Kubernetes	kube-state-metrics
integration,monitoring
kube-state-metrics connects to the Kubernetes API server to expose metrics about the state of Kubernetes objects.

Prometheus	kube-state-metrics
data collection,monitoring
Prometheus can scrape the endpoint of kube-state-metrics to collect metrics.

Pod	kubectl
command-line interface,pod management
kubectl is used to manage Pods, including deploying and fetching logs.

Container	Pod
containerization,deployment
A Pod can contain one or more Containers.

Pod	kube-state-metrics
metrics,monitoring
kube-state-metrics exposes metrics about the state of Pods, including readiness and termination status.

Container	kube_pod_container_info
container details,metrics
This metric provides detailed information about a container, including its name, image, and ID.

Pod	kube_pod_status_readycondition
metrics,pod status
This metric reflects the readiness status of a pod, crucial for monitoring and alerting.

Pod	kube_pod_deletion_timestamp
metrics,pod lifecycle
This metric tracks the deletion timestamp of a pod, useful for alerting on prolonged termination.

Container	Standard Output (stdout)
container behavior,logging,output stream,program output
Containers produce standard output as part of their normal operation.<SEP>Containers write logs to stdout for standard output.

Container	Standard Error (stderr)
container behavior,error reporting,error stream,logging
Containers produce standard error to report errors or exceptional conditions during execution.<SEP>Containers write error logs to stderr.

Container	PodLogsQuerySplitStreams
feature gate,logging
This feature gate allows for separate retrieval of stdout and stderr logs from containers.

Node	Pod
cluster architecture,deployment
Pods run on Nodes in a Kubernetes cluster.

Cluster-level logging	Node
logging architecture,scalability
Cluster-level logging separates log management from individual nodes for improved resilience and scalability.

PodLogsQuerySplitStreams	kubelet
feature implementation,system component
The kubelet utilizes the PodLogsQuerySplitStreams feature gate to control the log stream splitting behavior for containers.

Standard Output (stdout)	kubelet
container monitoring,log management
The kubelet, when PodLogsQuerySplitStreams is enabled, separates the container's standard output into a distinct log stream for better management and analysis.

Standard Error (stderr)	kubelet
error handling,log management
The kubelet, when PodLogsQuerySplitStreams is enabled, separates the container's standard error into a distinct log stream for better error handling and analysis.

Kubernetes	kubectl
tool-system
Kubectl is the command-line interface for interacting with Kubernetes clusters.

Kubernetes	ReplicaSets
resource-system
ReplicaSets are a Kubernetes resource for managing replicated pods.

DaemonSets	Kubernetes
resource-system
DaemonSets are a Kubernetes resource for managing pods that run on every node in a cluster.

Deployments	Kubernetes
resource-system
Deployments are a Kubernetes resource for managing the desired state of a set of pods.

Jobs	Kubernetes
resource-system
Jobs are a Kubernetes resource for managing finite tasks.

CronJobs	Kubernetes
resource-system
CronJobs are a Kubernetes resource for scheduling Jobs.

Kubernetes	ReplicationControllers
resource-system
ReplicationControllers are a Kubernetes resource for managing replicated pods (deprecated).

EndpointSlice	Service
component-relationship
A Service uses EndpointSlices to determine which endpoints are used for that Service.

Deployments	Pods
management-relationship
Deployments manage the desired state of a set of Pods.

Pods	ReplicaSets
management-relationship
ReplicaSets manage the desired state of a set of Pods.

Imperative Object Configuration	Kubernetes
implementation-method
Kubernetes supports imperative object configuration.

Declarative Object Configuration	Kubernetes
implementation-method
Kubernetes supports declarative object configuration.

Imperative Object Configuration	kubectl
tool-method
Kubectl can be used with imperative object configuration.

Declarative Object Configuration	kubectl
tool-method
Kubectl can be used with declarative object configuration (apply, diff commands).

Declarative Object Configuration	patch API operation
method-implementation
Declarative configuration uses the patch API to update objects.

Declarative Object Configuration	replace API operation
method-implementation
Declarative configuration can use the replace API, but patch is preferred for partial updates.

Dependent	Owner
dependency,lifecycle management,ownership
A Dependent's existence is contingent upon its Owner; the absence of the Owner may trigger the deletion of the Dependent.<SEP>An Owner has ownership over one or more Dependents.

Dependent	metadata.ownerReferences field
metadata
The metadata.ownerReferences field identifies the owner of a Dependent object.

Owner	ownerReferences.blockOwnerDeletion field
deletion-control
The ownerReferences.blockOwnerDeletion field controls whether a Dependent can block deletion of its Owner.

Kubernetes admission controller	ownerReferences.blockOwnerDeletion field
access-control
The Kubernetes admission controller manages access to modify the ownerReferences.blockOwnerDeletion field.

Pods	ReplicationControllers
management
ReplicationControllers manage Pods (deprecated).

Kubernetes	v1.20
feature change,software version
The described behavior change in garbage collection is specific to version 1.20 of Kubernetes.

Cluster-scoped Dependent	Cluster-scoped Owner
dependency,scope
Cluster-scoped Dependents can only specify Cluster-scoped Owners as their owners.

Cluster-scoped Dependent	Namespaced Kind
garbage collection,incompatibility
In v1.20, if a Cluster-scoped Dependent specifies a Namespaced Kind as an Owner, it's treated as having an unresolvable owner reference and cannot be garbage collected.

Namespace	Owner
containment,scope
Owners can exist within a specific Namespace, influencing the scope of their dependents.

Dependent	Namespace
containment,scope
Dependents can exist within a specific Namespace, potentially inheriting scoping rules from their Owners.

Garbage Collection	v1.20
feature impact,version change
Version 1.20 changed the behavior of garbage collection regarding cluster-scoped dependents and namespaced owners.

CSI driver	Kubernetes
integration,storage management
CSI drivers allow Kubernetes to interact with various storage systems.

CSI driver	kubelet
node communication,volume management
The kubelet interacts with the CSI driver to manage volumes on nodes.

CSI driver	kube-apiserver
API interaction,cluster management
The kube-apiserver interacts with the CSI driver through the Kubernetes API.

CSI driver	PersistentVolume
storage provisioning,volume lifecycle
PersistentVolumes are managed by CSI drivers.

CSI driver	PersistentVolumeClaim
storage request,volume allocation
PersistentVolumeClaims are fulfilled by PVs managed by CSI drivers.

CSI driver	Storage Class
configuration,storage provisioning
StorageClasses define parameters for creating PVs managed by CSI drivers.

CSI driver	in-tree plugin
migration,modernization
CSI drivers are replacing in-tree plugins for storage management.

CSI node plugin	csi-proxy
Windows support,privileged operations
csi-proxy facilitates privileged operations for CSI node plugins on Windows nodes.

CSI node plugin	Linux
deployment,operating system
CSI node plugins are typically deployed as privileged containers on Linux nodes.

CSI node plugin	Windows
Windows support,privileged operations
CSI node plugins require csi-proxy for privileged operations on Windows nodes.

CSI driver	CreateVolumeResponse
data exchange,driver interaction
The CreateVolumeResponse contains attributes defined by the CSI driver.

CSI driver	CSI spec
implementation,specification
CSI drivers must adhere to the CSI specification.

CSI driver	ControllerPublishVolumeRequest
API request,driver interaction
This request passes volume context to the CSI driver.

CSI driver	NodeStageVolumeRequest
API request,driver interaction
This request passes volume context to the CSI driver for staging.

CSI driver	NodePublishVolumeRequest
API request,driver interaction
This request passes volume context to the CSI driver for publishing.

CSI driver	ControllerPublishVolume
driver function,method call
This is a CSI method called by Kubernetes.

CSI driver	ControllerUnpublishVolume
driver function,method call
This is a CSI method called by Kubernetes.

CSI driver	NodeExpandVolume
driver function,method call
This is a CSI method called by Kubernetes for volume expansion.

CSI driver	NodeStageVolume
driver function,method call
This is a CSI method called by Kubernetes for volume staging.

CSI driver	NodePublishVolume
driver function,method call
This is a CSI method called by Kubernetes for volume publishing.

CSINodeExpandSecret	NodeExpandVolume
feature gate,secret management
This feature gate enables secret passing for NodeExpandVolume calls.

CSI driver	PersistentVolumePersistentVolumeClaim
storage,volume management
CSI drivers manage PersistentVolumes used by PersistentVolumeClaims.

CSI driver	Ephemeral Volumes
driver support,volume type
CSI drivers can support ephemeral volumes.

CSI driver	kubernetes-csi documentation
documentation,driver development
This documentation provides information on developing CSI drivers.

CSIMigration	in-tree plugin
migration,plugin replacement
CSIMigration facilitates the transition from in-tree plugins to CSI drivers.

CSI driver	PV
PV creation,storage management
The CSI driver manages the creation and management of Persistent Volumes (PVs).  The text explicitly states that PV creation works with a properly configured CSI driver.

CSIMigration	In-tree plugins
migration support,plugin functionality
In-tree plugins support CSIMigration, indicating a relationship between the plugin and the migration process.

CSI driver	In-tree plugins
CSIMigration,plugin-driver relationship
In-tree plugins that support CSIMigration have a corresponding CSI driver implemented, showing a direct link between the plugin and the driver.

Kubernetes	NodeRestriction
label management,security
Kubernetes utilizes the NodeRestriction admission plugin for enhanced security and control over node labels.

NodeRestriction	kubelet
label restriction,security
The NodeRestriction plugin prevents the kubelet from modifying labels with a specific prefix, enhancing security.

Node authorizer	NodeRestriction
authorization,label control
The Node authorizer works in conjunction with the NodeRestriction plugin to control label modifications on nodes.

NodeRestriction	Pod
label selection,pod scheduling
Pods can utilize node selectors to target nodes with specific labels, indirectly interacting with NodeRestriction's label restrictions.

Kubernetes	Node
cluster management
Kubernetes manages and orchestrates Nodes in a cluster.

Kubernetes	Pod
pod management
Kubernetes schedules and manages Pods across Nodes.

Labels	Node
node identification
Nodes are identified and categorized using labels.

Labels	Pod
pod selection
Pods can be selected and scheduled based on labels.

Pod	nodeSelector
pod scheduling
nodeSelector is used to constrain Pod scheduling based on Node labels.

Labels	nodeSelector
label filtering
nodeSelector uses Node labels to filter suitable Nodes for Pod scheduling.

Affinity	Labels
scheduling constraints
Affinity uses labels to define complex scheduling constraints for Pods.

Anti-affinity	Labels
pod placement
Anti-affinity uses labels to prevent co-location of Pods.

Labels	Node Affinity
node selection
Node Affinity uses Node labels to constrain Pod scheduling.

Inter-pod Affinity	Labels
pod co-location
Inter-pod Affinity uses Pod labels to constrain Pod scheduling.

Node Affinity	requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution
affinity rule type
requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution is a type of Node Affinity rule.

Node Affinity	preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution
affinity rule type
preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution is a type of Node Affinity rule.

Node	topology.kubernetes.io/zone
node location
Nodes are often labeled with topology.kubernetes.io/zone to indicate their location.

Node	another-node-label-key
custom labeling
Nodes can have custom labels like another-node-label-key for specific purposes.

data.content:
In, NotIn, Exists, DoesNotExist, Gt, Lt	nodeSelector
logical operations
nodeSelector uses logical operators to combine label constraints.

data.tgt_id: In, NotIn, Exists, DoesNotExist, Gt, Lt
data.content:
Affinity	In, NotIn, Exists, DoesNotExist, Gt, Lt
logical operations
Affinity uses logical operators to combine label constraints.

In	Kubernetes
node affinity,operator
Kubernetes uses the 'In' operator in its node affinity rules to specify label matching conditions.

Kubernetes	NotIn
node anti-affinity,operator
Kubernetes uses the 'NotIn' operator in its node affinity rules to specify label exclusion conditions.

Exists	Kubernetes
node affinity,operator
Kubernetes uses the 'Exists' operator in its node affinity rules to check for the presence of labels.

DoesNotExist	Kubernetes
node anti-affinity,operator
Kubernetes uses the 'DoesNotExist' operator in its node affinity rules to check for the absence of labels.

Gt	Kubernetes
node affinity,operator
Kubernetes uses the 'Gt' operator in its node affinity rules for numerical label value comparisons.

Kubernetes	Lt
node affinity,operator
Kubernetes uses the 'Lt' operator in its node affinity rules for numerical label value comparisons.

Kubernetes	Node Anti-affinity
deployment,feature
Kubernetes utilizes Node Anti-affinity to distribute pods across different nodes for high availability.

Kubernetes	Node Affinity
feature,resource allocation
Kubernetes employs Node Affinity to strategically place pods on nodes with specific characteristics.

Gateway API	Kubernetes
API specification,Kubernetes ecosystem
Kubernetes supports the Gateway API as a specification for managing ingress resources.

AWS	Kubernetes
Ingress controller support,cloud provider
Kubernetes supports the AWS Ingress controller.

GCE	Kubernetes
Ingress controller support,cloud provider
Kubernetes supports the GCE Ingress controller.

Kubernetes	nginx
Ingress controller support,open-source software
Kubernetes supports the nginx Ingress controller.

AKS Application Gateway Ingress Controller	Azure Application Gateway
Ingress management,cloud service
This Ingress controller configures the Azure Application Gateway.

Alibaba Cloud MSE Ingress	Alibaba Cloud Native Gateway
Ingress management,cloud service
This Ingress controller configures the Alibaba Cloud Native Gateway.

Alibaba Cloud Native Gateway	Higress
Commercial offering,open-source software
Alibaba Cloud Native Gateway is the commercial version of Higress.

Apache APISIX	Apache APISIX ingress controller
API gateway,Ingress implementation
This Ingress controller is based on Apache APISIX.

Avi Kubernetes Operator	VMware NSX Advanced Load Balancer
Load balancing,infrastructure
This operator uses VMware NSX Advanced Load Balancer for load balancing.

BFE	BFE Ingress Controller
Ingress implementation,network solution
This Ingress controller is based on BFE.

Cilium	Cilium Ingress Controller
Ingress implementation,networking solution
This Ingress controller is powered by Cilium.

Contour	Envoy
Ingress implementation,proxy technology
Contour is an Envoy-based Ingress controller.

Emissary-Ingress API Gateway	Envoy
Ingress implementation,proxy technology
Emissary-Ingress is an Envoy-based Ingress controller.

EnRoute	Envoy
API gateway,Ingress implementation,proxy technology
EnRoute is an Envoy-based API gateway that can function as an Ingress controller.

Easegress	Easegress IngressController
API gateway,Ingress implementation,product-platform relationship,software architecture
The Easegress IngressController is a product or implementation built upon the Easegress platform.<SEP>The Easegress IngressController is a specific product or implementation built upon the Easegress platform.<SEP>This Ingress controller is based on Easegress.

Ingress	Kubernetes
Kubernetes,Resource management
Ingress is a Kubernetes resource for routing external traffic.

Ingress	Ingress Controller
Implementation,functionality
An Ingress controller is required to implement the Ingress resource and route traffic.

Ingress Controller	Kubernetes
Component dependency,Kubernetes
Kubernetes clusters need an Ingress controller to function with Ingress resources.

Citrix Application Delivery Controller	Citrix ingress controller
Integration,application delivery
This controller integrates with Citrix ADC for application delivery.

Custom Resources	Gateway API
Implementation,extension
The Gateway API is implemented using Kubernetes Custom Resources.

Gateway API	Implementations
Specification,implementation
Multiple implementations exist for the Gateway API.

Easegress	Envoy
API gateway comparison,ingress controller functionality
Both Envoy and Easegress are API gateways that can function as ingress controllers; the text presents them as comparable options.

Envoy-based API gateway	httpsgetenroute.io
implementation,product-technology relationship
The URL likely points to an organization or product that utilizes an Envoy-based API gateway.

Envoy	Envoy-based API gateway
implementation,technology-product relationship
An Envoy-based API gateway is built using Envoy technology.

Envoy-based API gateway	ingress controller
functionality,role
An Envoy-based API gateway can function as an ingress controller.

Envoy	httpswww.envoyproxy.io
organization-website relationship
This URL likely points to the official website of the Envoy project.

Easegress IngressController	ingress controller
functionality,role
The Easegress IngressController functions as a Kubernetes ingress controller.

Easegress	httpsmegaease.comdocseaseasegress04.cloud-native4.1.kubernetes-ingress-controller
organization-documentation relationship
This URL likely points to documentation related to the Easegress Ingress Controller.

Easegress	httpsmegaease.comeasegress
organization-website relationship
This URL likely points to the official website of the Easegress project.

Easegress IngressController	Envoy-based API gateway
API gateway comparison,ingress controller functionality
Both are API gateways that function as ingress controllers, presented for comparison.
